evals.name: basic-single-text-block
evals.prompt: Create a scroll-driven video animation page with a single hero headline that fades in at 20% scroll progress and fades out at 40%. Include a simple navigation bar with a brand logo on the left and menu icon on the right.
evals.expected_output: A complete HTML file with a fixed video element, proper scroll-to-video mapping using requestAnimationFrame, one text block with data-start=0.2 and data-end=0.4, and a transparent navigation bar. The body height should be 600vh and all CSS/JS should be inline.

assertions.description: HTML file exists and contains video element
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert '<video' in html, 'Missing video element'
assert 'id="scrollVideo"' in html, 'Video element should have id scrollVideo'

assertions.description: Video has required attributes (muted, playsinline, preload, no autoplay/controls)
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert 'muted' in html, 'Video should be muted'
assert 'playsinline' in html, 'Video should have playsinline attribute'
assert 'preload="auto"' in html or "preload='auto'" in html, 'Video should preload auto'
assert 'autoplay' not in html, 'Should NOT have autoplay attribute'
assert 'controls' not in html, 'Should NOT have controls attribute'

assertions.description: Video CSS is correct (fixed position, cover, z-index 0)
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert 'position: fixed' in html, 'Video should have position fixed'
assert 'object-fit: cover' in html, 'Video should have object-fit cover'
assert 'z-index: 0' in html, 'Video should have z-index 0'

from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
import re
height_match = re.search(r'body\s*\{[^}]*height:\s*(\d+)vh', html)
assert height_match, 'Body should have height in vh'
height = int(height_match.group(1))
assert 500 <= height <= 600, f'Body height {height}vh should be between 500-600vh'

assertions.description: Text block exists with correct data attributes
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert 'class="text-block' in html or "class='text-block" in html, 'Should have text-block class'
assert 'data-start=' in html, 'Should have data-start attribute'
assert 'data-end=' in html, 'Should have data-end attribute'
# Check values
assert 'data-start="0.2"' in html or "data-start='0.2'" in html, 'Text block should start at 0.2'
assert 'data-end="0.4"' in html or "data-end='0.4'" in html, 'Text block should end at 0.4'

from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert 'requestAnimationFrame' in html, 'Should use requestAnimationFrame for smooth scrolling'
assert 'scrollY' in html, 'Should read scrollY position'
assert 'currentTime' in html, 'Should set video.currentTime'

assertions.description: Text blocks have will-change for GPU acceleration
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert 'will-change' in html, 'Should use will-change for GPU acceleration'
assert ('transform, opacity' in html) or ('opacity, transform' in html), 'Should have will-change for transform and opacity'

assertions.description: Navigation bar is fixed and transparent
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert '<nav' in html, 'Should have nav element'
assert 'position: fixed' in html, 'Nav should be fixed'
# z-index higher than video
assert 'z-index: 10' in html or 'z-index: 9' in html, 'Nav should have high z-index'

evals.name: multiple-text-blocks-offset
evals.prompt: Generate a scroll video page with three text blocks appearing at different scroll positions: first block aligned left from 10% to 25%, second aligned right from 35% to 50%, and third centered from 60% to 75%. Use alternating text alignment and proper offsets.
evals.expected_output: HTML file with three .text-block divs having appropriate position classes (left, right, center), data-start and data-end attributes matching the percentages, proper z-index layering, and smooth fade animations. The text should be offset from edges using padding.

assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
import re
blocks = re.findall(r'class="text-block[^"]*"', html)
assert len(blocks) >= 3, f'Should have at least 3 text blocks, found {len(blocks)}'

assertions.description: Text blocks have individual start/end ranges
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
import re
starts = re.findall(r'data-start="([0-9.]+)"', html) + re.findall(r"data-start='([0-9.]+)'", html)
ends = re.findall(r'data-end="([0-9.]+)"', html) + re.findall(r"data-end='([0-9.]+)'", html)
assert len(starts) >= 3, 'Should have at least 3 data-start values'
assert len(ends) >= 3, 'Should have at least 3 data-end values'
# Check ranges roughly match 0.1-0.25, 0.35-0.5, 0.6-0.75
floats = lambda vals: [float(v) for v in vals]
assert any(0.08 <= s <= 0.12 for s in floats(starts)), 'First block should start around 0.1'
assert any(0.33 <= s <= 0.37 for s in floats(starts)), 'Second block should start around 0.35'
assert any(0.58 <= s <= 0.62 for s in floats(starts)), 'Third block should start around 0.6'

assertions.description: Left, right, and center positioning classes present
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
has_left = 'left' in html and 'text-block left' in html
has_right = 'right' in html and 'text-block right' in html
has_center = 'center' in html and 'text-block center' in html
assert has_left, 'Should have left-aligned text block'
assert has_right, 'Should have right-aligned text block'
assert has_center, 'Should have centered text block'

from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
css_match = re.search(r'\.text-block\s*\{[^}]*\}', html)
assert css_match, 'Should have .text-block CSS rule'
css = css_match.group(0)
assert 'position: fixed' in css, 'Text blocks should be fixed position'

evals.name: mobile-optimized
evals.prompt: Create a scroll-video page optimized for mobile devices. Include responsive text sizing, appropriate padding for touch screens, and ensure iOS compatibility with playsinline. Add a media query for screens under 768px.
evals.expected_output: HTML file with video scroll animation plus mobile-responsive CSS using @media queries, reduced text sizes on mobile, increased padding, and all required video attributes for iOS. Should still follow core architecture principles.

assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert 'viewport' in html, 'Should have viewport meta tag'
assert 'width=device-width' in html, 'Viewport should set width=device-width'

assertions.description: Media query for mobile screens exists
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert '@media' in html, 'Should have media queries'
assert 'max-width: 768px' in html or 'max-width:768px' in html, 'Should target mobile breakpoint'

assertions.description: Video has playsinline attribute for iOS
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert 'playsinline' in html, 'Video must have playsinline for iOS'

assertions.description: Mobile styles adjust text size or padding
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
import re
mobile_css = re.search(r'@media[^{]+\{[^}]+\}', html, re.DOTALL)
assert mobile_css, 'Should have mobile media query block'
mobile_styles = mobile_css.group(0)
has_adjustment = 'font-size' in mobile_styles or 'padding' in mobile_styles or 'text' in mobile_styles
assert has_adjustment, 'Mobile media query should adjust layout'

assertions.description: Scroll animation logic exists and uses requestAnimationFrame
assertions.script:
from pathlib import Path
html = Path('index.html').read_text()
assert 'requestAnimationFrame' in html, 'Should use requestAnimationFrame'
assert 'addEventListener' in html and 'scroll' in html, 'Should have scroll event listener'
